video_title: Linear Algebra Foundations for Machine Learning
speaker: Dr. Markus Weber

chapters.title: Vectors and Vector Spaces
chapters.transcript: Vectors are the fundamental building blocks. A vector is an ordered list of numbers. In machine learning, each data point is often a vector. We will cover vector addition, scalar multiplication, and linear combinations.

chapters.title: Matrices and Linear Transformations
chapters.transcript: Matrices represent linear transformations. Multiplying a matrix by a vector transforms it. Matrix multiplication is associative but not commutative. We also cover identity and inverse matrices.

chapters.title: Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors
chapters.transcript: Eigenvectors are special directions that do not change under a transformation, only scaling by eigenvalues. Principal Component Analysis relies on eigendecomposition.

chapters.title: Norms and Inner Products
chapters.transcript: Norms measure vector length. L1 and L2 norms are common. Dot products measure similarity. Cauchy-Schwarz inequality is key.

chapters.title: Applications in ML
chapters.transcript: Linear regression, PCA, neural network weight matrices, and SVD for dimensionality reduction all depend on linear algebra.
